description:
You are a data-scientist who has inherited a messy project tree at
    
        /home/user/workspace/raw_data
    
    Before you can start analysing anything you have to tidy it up.  
    Only the standard GNU user-land tools available in a minimal Linux
    distribution may be used; **your solution must rely exclusively on
    a combination of “find … | xargs …” (with any supporting flags)
    together with the usual POSIX utilities such as rm, gzip, rmdir,
    wc, etc.**  A typical final command sequence should not exceed
    about ten individual commands.

    Initial directory layout and relevant files:

        /home/user/workspace/raw_data/
        ├── customer_data_2020.csv
        ├── customer_data_2020.csv.bak          (editor back-up)
        ├── product_data_2021.csv
        ├── old_notes.txt~                      (tilde back-up)
        ├── sales_Q4_2021.csv
        ├── README.md
        ├── logs/
        │   ├── usage.log
        │   ├── error.log~
        │   └── debug.log~
        ├── archive/
        │   ├── old/
        │   │   ├── legacy_data.csv.bak
        │   │   ├── placeholder.txt~
        │   │   └── empty_dir/                  (completely empty)
        │   └── zip/                            (completely empty)
        └── duplicates/
            └── duplicates_readme.txt           (kept for reference)

    Cleaning requirements
    ---------------------
    1. Delete *all* files whose names end in “.bak” **or** “~”.
    2. Find every remaining “*.csv” file and compress it with gzip,
       deleting the original uncompressed “.csv” when the compression
       succeeds.  The resulting files must therefore have the suffix
       “.csv.gz”.
    3. Remove every directory that is now completely empty as a result
       of steps 1-2.
    4. Create an ascii report file called

           /home/user/workspace/cleaning_report.txt

       The report *must* contain exactly three lines, each in the form
       KEY:VALUE (no additional spaces), terminated with a single
       newline.  The keys and the values you have to produce are:

           BACKUP_DELETED:<number_of_*.bak_and_~_files_removed>
           CSV_COMPRESSED:<number_of_*.csv_files_gzipped>
           EMPTY_DIR_REMOVED:<number_of_empty_directories_deleted>

       The automated tests compare this file byte-for-byte, so respect
       the exact spelling, order, capitalisation, the colon separator
       and the newline (LF) at the end of each line.

    Validation the test-suite will perform
    --------------------------------------
    • No file whose name ends in “.bak” or “~” may exist anywhere
      under /home/user/workspace/raw_data after you finish.
    • Exactly three *.csv.gz files must be present:
        customer_data_2020.csv.gz
        product_data_2021.csv.gz
        sales_Q4_2021.csv.gz
      Their uncompressed “.csv” counterparts must be gone.
    • The two now-useless directories
        /home/user/workspace/raw_data/archive/old/empty_dir
        /home/user/workspace/raw_data/archive/zip
      must have been deleted, while all non-empty directories (e.g.
      logs/, archive/, duplicates/) must remain.
    • /home/user/workspace/cleaning_report.txt must exist and follow
      the precise format described above.

    Hints (do NOT copy-paste, think first!)
    ---------------------------------------
    • The “-type f” and “-type d” predicates of “find” together with
      “-name” or “-regex” will be essential.
    • Remember that you can chain several calls to “find … -print0 |
      xargs -0 …” to handle file names containing spaces, although
      none of the provided names actually contain spaces.
    • The test harness will run your commands from an arbitrary
      working directory, so always use full or explicitly relative
      paths.

    Finish when the tree is clean and the report is present.
truth:
BACKUP_DELETED must be 6
    CSV_COMPRESSED must be 3
    EMPTY_DIR_REMOVED must be 2

    Precise expected final file/dir list under /home/user/workspace/raw_data:

        customer_data_2020.csv.gz
        product_data_2021.csv.gz
        sales_Q4_2021.csv.gz
        README.md
        logs/
            usage.log
        archive/
            old/
                (no files left here)
        duplicates/
            duplicates_readme.txt

    The file /home/user/workspace/cleaning_report.txt must contain (with \n as LF):

        BACKUP_DELETED:6\n
        CSV_COMPRESSED:3\n
        EMPTY_DIR_REMOVED:2\n

    Any existence of “*.bak”, “*~”, uncompressed “*.csv”, or the two
    originally empty directories causes failure.
